chunks.text: Media Mentions Archive - Page 2 of 5 - EECS at Berkeley Media Mentions June 12, 2025 May the Best Bot Win Started as student side project, Chatbot Arena is now the AI industry’s scoreboard—and a $100 million startup May 5, 2025 SF Chronicle: One UC major leads to the highest paying jobs — even more than the same degree elsewhere March 31, 2025 WIRED: Angelina Jolie Was Right About Computers David Patterson, professor emeritus of computer science, has reshaped modern computing with his groundbreaking work on RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) architecture. Wired explores how his early research at UC Berkeley laid the foundation for RISC-V, an open-source alternative now driving innovation across the industry. March 17, 2025 NPR: Researchers are rushing to build AI-powered robots. But will they work? EECS Professor Ken Goldberg and EECS Alumna Chelsea Finn (Ph.D. ’18) discuss the latest advancements in AI and their integration into robotics. February 12, 2025 20 UC Berkeley alumni, students and faculty shine on Forbes 30 Under 30 list EECS alumni named in Forbes 30 Under 30 list. January 30, 2025 NBC News: Ion Stoica on DeepSeek EECS Professor Ion Stoica discusses DeepSeek with Gadi Schwartz on NBC News NOW. January 17, 2025 Ion Stoica featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast EECS Professor and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica is featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast. December 6, 2024 The UC Berkeley Project That Is the AI Industry’s Obsession EECS grad students Anastasios Angelopoulos (advisors: Jordan and Malik) and Wei-Lin Chiang (advisor: Stoica) were profiled by The Wall Street Journal for their work on Chatbot Arena. November 25, 2024 Election Forecasts Try to Go Beyond the Polls. Are They Helping? UC Berkeley EECS Professor Benjamin Recht was recently featured in The New York Times for his critique of election forecasting models, emphasizing the complex decisions behind these predictions. His insights spotlight the intersection of statistical modeling and societal impact. « Previous Page 1 2 3 4 5 Next Page » View Open Faculty Positions
